questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the goddess Durga is best known as which kind of deity?
questions.explanation: Durga is venerated as the fierce protective warrior form of the Great Goddess, born to vanquish demons the gods could not defeat.

questions.q: Which animal is most commonly depicted as the mount (vahana) of the goddess Durga?
questions.explanation: Durga is traditionally depicted riding a lion or tiger as her vahana in battle.

questions.q: According to the Devi Mahatmya, the goddess Durga was formed from what source?
options: The combined radiance and energies of the devas
The churning of the ocean of milk
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya relates that Durga arose from the combined radiance and energies of the assembled gods.

questions.q: The goddess Durga is most often depicted with how many arms?
options: Many arms, each bearing a weapon
questions.explanation: Iconography from the Devi Mahatmya depicts Durga with many arms, each bearing a weapon gifted by the gods.

questions.q: The weapons borne by the goddess Durga were, according to the Devi Mahatmya, obtained how?
options: Gifted to her by the various gods
questions.explanation: Each of Durga's many weapons was gifted to her by a different deity to equip her for battle.

questions.q: Which trident-bearing god is the consort of Parvati, of whom Durga is a fierce form?
questions.explanation: Durga is a fierce form of Parvati, the consort of Shiva.

questions.q: Which festival is especially associated with the worship of the goddess Durga?
questions.explanation: Durga is celebrated above all during Navaratri, the festival of nine nights honoring the goddess.

questions.q: The buffalo-demon slain by the goddess Durga is known by what name?
questions.explanation: Durga slew the buffalo-demon Mahishasura, earning the name Mahishasuramardini.

questions.q: According to the Devi Mahatmya, the goddess Durga was created to defeat a demon who could not be overcome by whom?
questions.explanation: Durga was created from the combined energies of the devas to vanquish a demon that the gods themselves could not defeat.

questions.q: The goddess Durga is regarded as a form of which Great Goddess, the daughter of the mountain?
questions.explanation: Durga is counted among the fierce forms of Parvati, the daughter of the mountain Himavan.

questions.q: The principal scripture describing the goddess Durga's exploits, the Devi Mahatmya, is embedded within which Purana?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya, which recounts Durga's deeds, is contained within the Markandeya Purana.

questions.q: The name Mahishasuramardini, applied to the goddess Durga, means which of the following?
questions.explanation: Mahishasuramardini means 'slayer of the buffalo-demon Mahishasura,' the title Durga earned in battle.

questions.q: Which trident, the signature weapon of Shiva, is among those wielded by the goddess Durga against Mahishasura?
questions.explanation: Durga receives Shiva's Trishula (trident) and pierces Mahishasura with it.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, the demon Mahishasura was granted a boon making him invulnerable to which class of foes?
questions.explanation: Mahishasura's boon made him unconquerable by any man or god, which is why the goddess Durga was created to slay him.

questions.q: The fierce dark goddess said to emerge from the brow of Durga during battle is known as whom?
questions.explanation: Kali is said to have emerged from Durga's brow during the battle against the demons Chanda and Munda.

questions.q: Within the broader Shakta tradition, the goddess Durga is understood as an expression of which underlying cosmic principle?
questions.explanation: Durga is venerated as a manifestation of Shakti, the dynamic feminine cosmic energy revered in Shaktism.

questions.q: Which goddess of fortune, consort of Vishnu, is a distinct deity and NOT a fierce form of Durga?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, goddess of fortune and consort of Vishnu, is a distinct deity, whereas Kali and Chamunda are fierce forms associated with Durga.

questions.q: The goddess Durga is described in the Devi Mahatmya as having what demeanor even amid the fury of battle?
questions.explanation: Durga is depicted as serene yet unconquerable in battle, calm even as she vanquishes her foes.

questions.q: The festival of Navaratri, sacred to the goddess Durga, spans how many nights?
questions.explanation: Navaratri, dedicated to Durga, is the festival of nine nights, as its name indicates.

questions.q: Which conch-bearing preserver god contributed his energy and weapon to the creation of the goddess Durga in the Devi Mahatmya?
questions.explanation: Vishnu was among the devas whose energies coalesced into Durga and who gifted her his weapon.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, the demon Mahishasura was able to do what during his duel with the goddess Durga?
questions.explanation: Mahishasura was a shape-shifting demon, changing forms during his battle with Durga before she slew him.

questions.q: The goddess Durga belongs principally to which devotional tradition of Hinduism?
questions.explanation: Durga is a central goddess of Shaktism, the tradition that venerates the Great Goddess as supreme.

questions.q: Texts addressing the goddess Durga praise her supreme aspect as the primordial source from whom all goddesses emanate, often titled how?
questions.explanation: In Shakta theology Durga is identified with the Adi Parashakti, the primordial supreme power from whom all goddesses emanate.

questions.q: Which Purana, distinct from the Markandeya Purana, is a major Shakta text that elaborates the theology and exploits of the goddess?
questions.explanation: The Devi Bhagavata Purana is a major Shakta scripture elaborating the supremacy and deeds of the goddess alongside the Devi Mahatmya.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, the fierce form arising from Durga slays the demon Raktabija by what means?
options: Binding him with a celestial noose
Burning him with the third eye
Drinking each drop of his blood before it falls
questions.explanation: Kali, the fierce form arising from Durga, drank every drop of Raktabija's blood before it could touch the ground and multiply.

questions.q: The demons Chanda and Munda, slain in the Devi Mahatmya, give the goddess which epithet derived from their names?
questions.explanation: From slaying Chanda and Munda the fierce goddess gains the epithet Chamunda.

questions.q: The Devi Mahatmya is traditionally framed as a discourse recited to whom?
options: King Suratha and the merchant Samadhi by the sage Medha
The sage Markandeya to his disciples
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya is framed as the sage Medha narrating the goddess's glory to the exiled King Suratha and the merchant Samadhi.

questions.q: The very name 'Durga' is traditionally explained as conveying which meaning?
options: The one who is gentle and fair
The fort, or one difficult to approach or assail
questions.explanation: The name Durga is traditionally read as 'the fort' or 'one who is difficult to approach or assail,' reflecting her invincibility.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya, before facing Mahishasura the goddess also confronts which pair of demon brothers?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya recounts the goddess's battles against the demon brothers Shumbha and Nishumbha among her major exploits.

questions.q: Which weapon, the spinning discus of Vishnu, is among the divine arms the gods bestow upon the goddess Durga in the Devi Mahatmya?
questions.explanation: Vishnu bestows a discus like his Sudarshana Chakra upon Durga among the weapons gifted to her by the gods.
